coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in should test whether your Senior Engineer is making deliberate priority tradeoffs and surfacing delivery risk early enough for others to act.
openingQuestion: What are you deliberately saying no to right now so your next two weeks stay aligned with the team’s biggest bets?
listenFor: whether they name a specific project or request they are declining, deferring, or absorbing anyway
whether they mention a concrete dependency, owner, or decision date that could stall delivery
if they describe the last stakeholder update with specifics on tradeoffs, timing, or changed expectations
avoid: do not turn the check-in into a status readout of every task
do not jump straight to fixing blockers before testing how they are prioritising and signalling risk
goodOutcome: You and Test have agreed the one highest-leverage scope your Senior Engineer will protect over the next two weeks and the trigger for escalating slippage or stakeholder risk.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where work slipped or a stakeholder may have been surprised so you can test patterns without making the conversation abstract.
